key_themes: Operational progress and infrastructure build-out at Red Setter (new access road to Nifty Copper Mine, new camp setup)
Confirmation of gold-copper mineralization from prior drilling with major 2026 follow-up program planned (~9,000m, 25 holes)
Strategic tenement expansion near major producers (Telfer gold mine)
Attractive entry valuation amid gold price strength and logistical cost savings for 2026 exploration
Speculative junior miner upside with steady news flow but volatile share price history
summary: Wishbone Gold (WSBN.L), a small-cap Australian gold-copper explorer, is experiencing mildly positive sentiment driven by consistent operational updates and preparations for an active 2026 field season. Key positives include confirmed mineralization at Red Setter, a new access road and camp that will cut costs and improve logistics via proximity to the Nifty mine, plus a strategic tenement win near the Telfer gold mine. While the share price has pulled back significantly from 2025 highs (now trading ~32p with a ~£9.7M market cap), engaged investors and influencers view current levels as a buying opportunity ahead of assay results and drilling, though discussion volume remains niche and typical for a junior miner.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/WSBN.L/)[[2]](https://www.tradingview.com/news/urn:summary_document_report:quartr.com:3230152:0-wsbn-over-4-800-metres-drilled-at-red-setter-new-access-road-to-nifty-to-boost-2026-exploration/)[[3]](https://www.share-talk.com/wishbone-gold-awarded-key-tenement-near-telfer-drilling-plans-for-2026/)
sources: Company X account (@WishboneGoldPlc) and ~10 relevant investor posts (Apr 2026), 5+ recent news/RNS items (Feb–Apr 2026: tenement win, mineralization confirmation, 4,800m drilled update, road/camp progress), Stock pages and quotes (Yahoo Finance, LSE, Google Finance, London Stock Exchange), Share chat references (ADVFN, LSE.co.uk)
